mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack triggers immediate profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal collapsing aggregate demand, forcing equity repricing downward over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rising above 105 during supply-constrained regimes forces capacity utilization to peak, triggering inflationary expectations that depress long-horizon equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-INDPRO periods within the specified regime, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside the defined regime window.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation above 300 on the CPIAUCSL index forces aggressive central bank tightening, which immediately increases equity risk premiums and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth combined with falling unemployment rates indicates an overheating labor market, prompting immediate equity sell-offs as wage-push inflation fears materialize.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive 5-day returns in >65% of occurrences, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over individual series in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP directly expands corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of positive GDPC1 growth periods, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.10 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline index level during a recessionary regime signals severe output contraction, which triggers defensive equity positioning and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances within the recessionary regime, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.
